macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indices Close Lower Amid Weakening Trends
items.summary: All major U.S. indexes including S&P 500, Nasdaq-100, Dow Jones, and Russell 2000 closed lower on March 14, 2026, with nearly every major market ETF breaking below its 50-day moving average and approaching the 200-day moving average, signaling potential trend shifts.[1][2][5]
items.source: Trading Momentum / Grand Pinnacle Tribune / Morningstar

items.title: S&P 500 Down 1.60%, Nasdaq 1.26%, Dow 1.98% on Inflation and Energy Concerns
items.summary: Wall Street indices fell sharply on March 14, 2026, driven by latest inflation data, volatile energy prices, and uncertainty over Federal Reserve moves, with financial stocks like HSBC underperforming.[2]

items.title: FTSE 100 Ends Week Down 0.23% Amid Iran War Volatility
items.summary: UK's FTSE 100 closed down 0.43% on Friday at 10,261, marking a second weekly decline influenced by Iran war escalation, rising oil prices near $100, and warnings of £344 spike in energy bills.[3]

items.title: Markets Approach Critical 200-Day Moving Averages as Panic Builds
items.summary: Major US indexes hover near 200-day moving averages, with rising VIX fear gauge and sector rotations reversing; analysts warn of potential selling acceleration amid Iran war inflation fears.[1][5]

items.title: S&P 500 Faces Valuation Concerns at Historic Highs Despite Recent Declines
items.summary: S&P 500's decade-long surge hits high Shiller P/E ratios amid tech leadership recovery, but caution prevails with small-caps erasing YTD gains post-Iran conflict.[4][5]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Ethereum Network Activity Explodes with Record Staking and L2 Growth
items.summary: Ethereum ecosystem shows massive growth with monthly transaction count approaching 1.1 billion, up significantly year-over-year on Base (43%), Polygon (161%), and Arbitrum (88%). Nearly 31% of ETH supply is now staked at an all-time high, signaling strong conviction amid consolidating prices.[1]

items.title: Bittensor (TAO) Stages Massive Takeover Amid AI-Crypto Surge
items.summary: Bittensor (TAO) is experiencing aggressive price pumps from $140 to $230, drawing attention from retail and institutions like Silbert, who expanded its trust for institutional access. Positioned as a leader in decentralized AI compute competing with centralized labs.[2]

items.title: Bitcoin Holds at $71,000 After Dip to Low $60Ks Amid Institutional Buying
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading around $71,000 after dropping to the low $60,000 range, with institutions like BlackRock and others quietly accumulating despite past skepticism. Cycle analysis predicts a bottom around $40,000 this cycle and new highs by 2029.[4]

items.title: Europe's Largest Banks Adopt Crypto; Virtune Launches Krypto ETPs on Warsaw Stock Exchange
items.summary: Europe's largest banks are adopting crypto, boosting legitimacy. Virtune introduces regulated Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, and Solana ETPs traded in PLN on GPW, enabling easy exposure without crypto wallets.[1][6]

items.title: Morgan Stanley Applies for Crypto Custody License as Institutions Pile In
items.summary: Morgan Stanley files for a crypto custody license while strategies continue buying billions in Bitcoin. Bitcoin and other crypto ETFs attract major institutional capital amid ongoing market consolidation.[6]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Nemotron 3 Super: 120B Hybrid Mamba-Transformer MoE Model
items.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Super, a 120B parameter hybrid Mamba-Transformer Mixture of Experts model with 12B active parameters, 1M context length, and 2.2x throughput compared to GPT-OSS-120B. Details include pricing, benchmarks, and API access.

items.title: Elon Musk Predicts AI Will Make Jobs 'Optional' in the Future
items.summary: Elon Musk stated that advances in AI, machine learning, robotics, and automation could make jobs optional, reshaping global labor markets. Analysis shows high AI exposure for tech roles like software development.

items.title: US Mayors Resist Data Center Expansion Amid Growing AI Backlash
items.summary: US mayors are pushing back against the data center boom driven by AI demands, citing concerns over polluting gas turbines and shifting from promised job benefits ahead of midterm elections.

items.title: Tech Entrepreneur Develops AI-Designed mRNA Vaccine for Dog's Cancer
items.summary: Paul Conyngham used a chatbot to brainstorm and develop an AI-designed mRNA vaccine for his dog Rosie, diagnosed with deadly mast cell cancer.

items.title: Post-Training Advances in 2026: GRPO, DAPO, RLVR Replace RLHF
items.summary: A technical survey details how GRPO, DAPO, RLVR, and synthetic self-play have become the dominant post-training methods, replacing RLHF in AI model development.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sword Art Online: Echoes of Aincrad Announced for July 2026 Release
items.summary: A new action RPG set in the Sword Art Online universe is launching July 10, 2026 on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC. The game features character creation, real-time combat, partner synergy mechanics, and a hardcore 'Death Game Mode' that explores the series' darker themes.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Google Closes $32B Wiz Acquisition — Largest VC-Backed Deal Ever
items.summary: Google completed its $32 billion acquisition of Wiz, setting a new record for VC-backed exits and signaling rising premiums for AI-native cloud security infrastructure.

items.title: Rubin Ritter Appointed as Interim CEO of Kinnevik
items.summary: Board member Rubin Ritter has been appointed as interim CEO of Kinnevik effective immediately, replacing Georgi Ganev. Ritter, former co-CEO of Zalando, will oversee a comprehensive assessment of the company's operations and portfolio.

items.title: Southwest Airlines to End Service at Two Major Airports
items.summary: Southwest Airlines announced it will end service from two major airports, marking a significant shift in the airline's route network.
